note_data.title: Coordinate Geometry
keyPoints: The **gradient** of a straight line passing through points (x₁, y₁) and (x₂, y₂) is m = (y₂ - y₁)/(x₂ - x₁)
The equation of a straight line is **y = mx + c** where m is gradient and c is y-intercept
**Parallel lines** have equal gradients: m₁ = m₂
**Perpendicular lines** have gradients where m₁ × m₂ = -1
The **distance** between two points (x₁, y₁) and (x₂, y₂) is d = √[(x₂ - x₁)² + (y₂ - y₁)²]
The **midpoint** of a line segment joining (x₁, y₁) and (x₂, y₂) is ((x₁ + x₂)/2, (y₁ + y₂)/2)
The general equation of a **circle** with centre (a, b) and radius r is (x - a)² + (y - b)² = r²
A **tangent to a circle** at any point is perpendicular to the radius at that point
The equation **x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0** represents a circle with centre (-g, -f) and radius √(g² + f² - c)

definitions.definition: The measure of steepness of a line, calculated as the ratio of vertical change to horizontal change
A straight line that touches a circle at exactly one point
Two lines that intersect at right angles (90°)
The distance from the centre of a circle to any point on its circumference

howToAnswer.instruction: Calculate the exact value using appropriate formula and show working clearly
Prove the given result by substituting values and demonstrating the equality
Find the answer through calculation or logical reasoning with full working

modelAnswer.question: The line y = 2x + 3 is perpendicular to line L at point P(1, 5). Find the equation of line L.
modelAnswer.answer: Gradient of given line = 2. Since lines are perpendicular, gradient of L × 2 = -1. Therefore gradient of L = -1/2. Using y - y₁ = m(x - x₁): y - 5 = -1/2(x - 1). Simplifying: y = -1/2x + 9/2
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Substituting point P(1, 5) into equation

commonMistakes: Never confuse perpendicular condition m₁ × m₂ = -1 with parallel condition m₁ = m₂
Students often forget to use the distance formula correctly - always square before adding under the square root
When expanding circle equations, students miss the factor of 2 in 2gx and 2fy terms
Never substitute coordinates incorrectly - always check which is x-coordinate and which is y-coordinate
Students often forget that radius must be positive when finding circle equations
examinerTips: Coordinate geometry appears in every Paper 2 - usually worth 8-12 marks total
Always draw a clear diagram showing points, lines and circles with proper labels
Check your answer by substituting coordinates back into your equation
Use exact values (fractions/surds) unless specifically asked to round
formulas: Line equation: y - y₁ = m(x - x₁)
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Coordinate plane showing a circle with centre marked, radius drawn, and a tangent line touching at one point with the radius perpendicular to the tangent at point of contact
